other_observers: Reported to me by Gary Lefko who said the bird had been there for a couple weeks already.; I think Gary Lefko was the original observer.
specific_location: westernmost Wellington SWA pond along 64 Road
description: Dabbling duck identical in shape and size as the abundant American Wigeon (Anas americana) that were on the same pond. This individual, however, had a rusty-colored head with cream-colored crown stripe; I noticed a very faint iridescent green patch just behind the eye - faint enough that it was not obvious in all light/head angles; a peach-colored breast; light gray sides, wings, and back; the tertials as seen on the folded wing were boldly black-centered; the vent region was also black; the short bill was grayish with a large black tip.  The poor photo was taken during a snow squall, but it shows the head and crown stripe coloration.Behaviors: It swam with American Wigeons for the time I was there.Call: none heardPlumage: adult alternate
